name: Chunking Repertoire — 45 min
description: Structured session for learning or polishing one piece. Map chunks, work priority ones, iterate transitions, assemble.

items.label: Warmup (5 min)
items.instruction: Loose hands. Something you can already play. Doesn't need to relate to the piece.

items.label: Pick the piece + map chunks
items.instruction: Open the score (or your ear-transcribed sketch). Bracket the natural chunks — 4-8 measures each, ending at phrase boundaries. Number them: Chunk 1, 2, 3... A 32-bar tune is typically 4 chunks of 8 bars.

items.label: Baseline each chunk
items.instruction: Play each chunk in isolation, straight through. Mark which are already clean (green) and which need work (red). Prioritize the reds.

items.label: Work priority chunk #1 (Slow Practice on it)
items.instruction: Apply Slow Practice to the highest-priority chunk. Slow it down. Get it clean. Nudge tempo. 5 clean in a row at target = done. See the Slow Practice article for the full protocol.

items.label: Work priority chunk #2 (Slow Practice on it)
items.instruction: Same protocol on the second-priority chunk.

items.label: Work the transitions
items.instruction: Play the last measure of chunk N + the first measure of chunk N+1. Only that. Loop it. Get it clean. Move to the next transition. For 8 chunks there are 7 transitions — prioritize the ones between the chunks you just worked.

items.label: Assemble incrementally
items.instruction: Chunks 1 + 2 with the transition. Then 1 + 2 + 3. Then 1 + 2 + 3 + 4. Each time you add a chunk, if it breaks, drop back and work that transition. Do NOT play from the top if you break in bar 22.

items.label: Reflect
items.instruction: Which chunks are now green? Which transitions are still red? That's tomorrow's list.
